levels.name: BUILDING C
levels.scope: PLANT ROGUE DEVICE ON RACK 4. PHOTOGRAPH OPPORTUNISTIC FINDINGS.

venue.smokersDoor: Fire door on the south elevation

roe: No forced entry. Doors open by schedule, badge or neglect.
If challenged, present the letter of authorisation and withdraw.
Detentions are reported to the client. Keep them off the invoice.
intel: Staff prop the south fire door open on smoking breaks. Its lamp shows green while it stands open.
Reception staff badge colleagues through without a second look. Stay within two seconds of a badge-holder.
Deliveries leave the dock shutter unattended in repeating windows.
Site CCTV reports to a security office console that is rarely watched.

hints.text: STAFF PROP THIS FIRE DOOR ON THEIR BREAKS. THE LAMP SHOWS WHEN.
STAFF BADGE THROUGH HERE. SLIP IN CLOSE BEHIND THEM.
THIS BREAKER BLINDS THE SERVER CAMERAS FOR A SPELL. THE REST STAY LIVE.
AN UNATTENDED CONSOLE. LOOP A FEED AND ITS CAMERA SEES YESTERDAY.

levels.name: DATA CENTRE
levels.scope: PLANT ROGUE DEVICE IN THE HOSTING CAGE. PHOTOGRAPH OPPORTUNISTIC FINDINGS.

venue.plantTarget: the customer rack in the hosting cage

roe: No forced entry. No interference with customer hosting beyond the agreed device.
If challenged, present the letter of authorisation and withdraw.
A site lockdown ends the covert approach. Avoid triggering one.
intel: The building runs dark and the CCTV coverage is near-total. Treat darkness as cover and camera arcs as weather.
The NOC console loops a feed so its camera swears old footage is live. Charges are few, spend them where a camera guards the only way through.
The breaker kills one camera circuit, briefly. The others keep watching.
The hosting cage is restricted at all hours. No cover story survives inside it.

hints.text: CAMERAS OWN THESE CORRIDORS. DARKNESS IS COVER, USE IT.
THE NOC MULTIPLEXER. LOOP A FEED BEFORE YOU CROSS ITS CAMERA.
THE BREAKER KILLS ONE CIRCUIT. THE OTHER CAMERAS KEEP WATCHING.
THE HOSTING CAGE IS RESTRICTED. NOBODY BELONGS IN THERE, INCLUDING YOU.

levels.name: DISTRIBUTION WAREHOUSE
levels.scope: PLANT ROGUE DEVICE ON THE COMMS RACK. PHOTOGRAPH OPPORTUNISTIC FINDINGS.

venue.plantTarget: the comms rack in the secure cage

roe: No forced entry. No contact with goods in transit.
If challenged, present the letter of authorisation and withdraw.
Keep clear of vehicle routes on the dispatch apron.
intel: Heavy staff traffic on the floor. Contractor hi-vis is accepted at a distance without question.
The vest means nothing up close, inside the cage or the office, or once security is alarmed.
Racking blocks sightlines both ways. The floor is a maze that works for whoever moves quietly.
Spare vests hang in the break room and on the dispatch dock.

hints.text: CONTRACTOR HI-VIS. AT A DISTANCE, A VEST IS A REASON TO BE HERE.
A SPARE VEST ON THE DOCK. EYES SLIDE PAST HI-VIS OUT HERE.
THE SECURE CAGE. THE VEST STOPS WORKING PAST THAT DOOR.
THE OFFICE IS RESTRICTED. HI-VIS FOOLS NOBODY IN THERE.
